elements.sent-2411-001: We derive the full next-to-eikonal (NEik) corrections to the gluon propagator from before to after traversing a highly boosted gluon background field, including corrections both beyond the shockwave limit and beyond the static limit in particular.
elements.sent-2411-002: After summarizing the results of the full NEik corrections to the before-to-after quark propagator computed in our earlier works, we also derive the before-to-inside, inside-to-inside and inside-to-after quark and gluon propagators, which are building blocks to calculate high-energy scattering processes at NEik order.
elements.sent-2411-003: Using these results and also including the NEik corrections that stem from interactions with the target via t-channel quark exchanges, we compute inclusive cross sections for quark and gluon production at forward rapidities in quark-nucleus and gluon-nucleus scatterings at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-004: The Color Glass Condensate (CGC) (see \cite{Gelis:2010nm,Albacete:2014fwa,Blaizot:2016qgz for recent reviews and references therein) is the effective theory that describes the high energy limit of the hadronic collisions.}
elements.sent-2411-005: CGC relies on gluon saturation phenomena that can be reached at sufficiently high scattering energies.
elements.sent-2411-006: The increase in energy is provided by decreasing $x$ where $x$ is the longitudinal momentum fraction carried by the interacting partons.
elements.sent-2411-007: With decreasing $x$ the gluon density of the interacting hadrons increase rapidly and at sufficiently high energies (or sufficiently low values of $x$) this increase is tamed by nonlinear interactions of the emitted gluons and causes the aforementioned gluon saturation phenomena that is characterized by a dynamical scale known as saturation momenta $Q_s$.
elements.sent-2411-008: The evolution in $x$ is governed by the famous Balitsky-Kovchegov/Jalilian-Marian-Iancu-McLerran-Weigert-Leonidov-Kovner (BK-JIMWLK) equation derived in \cite{Balitsky:1995ub,Kovchegov:1999yj,Kovchegov:1999ua,Jalilian-Marian:1996mkd,Jalilian-Marian:1997qno,Jalilian-Marian:1997jhx,Jalilian-Marian:1997ubg,Kovner:2000pt,Weigert:2000gi,Iancu:2000hn,Iancu:2001ad,Ferreiro:2001qy .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Over the last three decades a vast amount of effort has been devoted to advance the CGC framework with the motivation of understanding the high energy collision data.
elements.sent-2411-010: Single inclusive particle/jet production at forward rapidities in proton-nucleus (pA) collisions is one of the observables that is frequently used to test the compatibility of saturation phenomena with the pA collision data from the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC) and the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).
elements.sent-2411-011: The calculation framework for this observable is known as "hybrid factorization" \cite{Dumitru:2005gt .}
elements.sent-2411-012: This approach allows one to treat the dilute projectile in the spirit of collinear factorization while the scattering of the projectile partons on the dense target is accounted for via eikonal approximation in the CGC framework.
elements.sent-2411-013: Even though the CGC framework have shown a great success in phenomenological studies of the high energy collisions, one needs to increase the precision of CGC computations of observables in order to achieve reliable quantitative description of the experimental data.
elements.sent-2411-014: This can be achieved either by computing the next-to-leading order (NLO) corrections in coupling constant $\alpha_s$ to observables or by relaxing the kinematical approximations adopted to compute the leading order (LO) observables.
elements.sent-2411-015: The NLO corrections to single inclusive particle/jet production at forward rapidity in pA collisions have been computed analytically \cite{Altinoluk:2011qy,Chirilli:2011km,Chirilli:2012jd,Stasto:2014sea,Kang:2014lha,Altinoluk:2014eka,Altinoluk:2015vax,Iancu:2016vyg,Liu:2020mpy,Ducloue:2016shw,Shi:2021hwx,Wang:2022zdu,Altinoluk:2023hfz .}
elements.sent-2411-016: Numerical studies of these results have been also performed \cite{Stasto:2013cha,Stasto:2014sea,Watanabe:2015tja,Ducloue:2017dit,Ducloue:2017mpb to test the compatibility with the experimental data.}
elements.sent-2411-017: As stated earlier, a complementary way to increase the precision of the CGC calculations is to relax the kinematic approximations adopted when computing the LO observables.
elements.sent-2411-018: High energy dilute-dense collisions within the CGC framework relies on two main approximations.
elements.sent-2411-019: The first one is referred to as the "semi-classical approximation".
elements.sent-2411-020: This approximation amounts to representing the dense target in the scattering process by a strong semiclassical background field $\A^{\mu _a(x)$.}
elements.sent-2411-021: The second approximation adopted within in the CGC is the well known eikonal approximation.
elements.sent-2411-022: Eikonal approximation amounts to keeping the leading power terms in energy in the high-energy limit and discarding the finite energy corrections.
elements.sent-2411-023: In the CGC framework, the high-energy limit can be achieved by boosting the target along $x^-$ with a boosting parameter $\gamma_t$.
elements.sent-2411-024: In this limit, the components of the semiclassical background field representing the target are scaled with the boosting parameter $\gamma_t$: \begin{equation}\label{eq_-_comp} \A^-(x)\propto \gamma_t\gg 1 \label{eq_j_comp} \A^j(x)\propto (\gamma_t)^0=1 \label{eq_+_comp} \A^+(x)\propto 1/ \gamma_t \ll 1\end{equation} Moreover, the coordinate dependence of the target fields is also scaled as \begin{equation}(x)\to \Big(\gamma_t x^+, \frac{x^-}{\gamma_t},\x\Big)\end{equation} in the high-energy limit that is achieved by boosting the target in the $x^-$ direction by a large boost parameter $\gamma_t$.
elements.sent-2411-025: In this setup, the eikonal approximation can be understood as the infinite boost of the target field $\A^{\mu (x)$ that amounts to the following three assumptions: (i) The background field $\A^{\mu (x)$ is assumed to be independent of $x^-$ coordiante due to the Lorentz time dilation.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: This is known as the static limit of the target fields and in this case there is no longitudinal momentum $p^+$ transfer from the target to the projectile during the interaction.
elements.sent-2411-027: This is know as the shockwave limit and in this limit partons from the projectile interact instantly in $x^+$ with the target without having any transverse motion within the target.
elements.sent-2411-028: The Eikonal approximation amounts to accounting for the interaction of the largest component of the background field $\A^-(x)$ and discarding the interactions of the transverse and $+$ components of the background field with the projectile partons.
elements.sent-2411-029: All in all, in the eikonal limit the background field takes the following form \begin{equation}\A^{\mu}(x^+,x^-,\x)\simeq \delta^{\mu-}\A^-(x^+,\x)\propto \delta(x^+)\end{equation} and as will be discussed in more detail in the next section, it resums the interactions of $\big(g\A^-(x^+,\x)\big)^n$ to all orders which leads to Wilson lines along $x^+$ direction.
elements.sent-2411-030: In order to consider the next-to-eikonal (NEik) corrections one should take into account the corrections that are of the order of $1/\gamma_t$ at the level of the boosted background field.
elements.sent-2411-031: These corrections can arise from relaxing either of the three approximations stated above.
elements.sent-2411-032: For the purpose of going beyond the static limit of the target fields (referred to as assumption (i) in the above discussion) one includes the $x^-$ dependence of the background field which goes beyond the infinite Lorentz time dilation.
elements.sent-2411-033: Indeed this correction is treated as a gradient expansion around a common $x^-$ which gives an order $1/\gamma_t$ effect.
elements.sent-2411-034: Another way to relax the eikonal approximation is to go beyond the so called shockwave limit and consider a finite longitudinal width target along $x^+$ direction (referred to as assumption (ii) in the above discussion).
elements.sent-2411-035: In the rest of the manuscript, in order to make the power counting more intuitive, we consider a finite support from $-L^+/2$ to $L^+/2$ for the target fields with a total longitudinal width $L^+$ of the target.
elements.sent-2411-036: Under the large boost this length $L^+$ is of order $1/\gamma_t$ due to Lorentz contraction.
elements.sent-2411-037: Nevertheless, our results obtained with the assumption of a finite support for the background field remain valid if the background field goes to zero faster than a power for $x^+\rightarrow \pm \infty$.
elements.sent-2411-038: Finally, the last source of the NEik corrections is to include the interactions of the transverse components of the background field with the projectile partons.
elements.sent-2411-039: This would go beyond the assumption (iii) listed above.
elements.sent-2411-040: Typically, this corresponds to replacing an enhanced $\A^-(x)$ insertion with an non-enhanced $\A^j(x)$ along the $x^+$ direction which would provide a $1/\gamma_t$ correction compared to the eikonal contribution.
elements.sent-2411-041: The above discussion for the sources of the NEik corrections is valid in the presence of a pure gluon background field.
elements.sent-2411-042: There is yet another distinct source of NEik corrections which originates from the interaction of the projectile parton and the target via t-channel quark exchange, which can be accounted for by including a quark background field as well for the target.
elements.sent-2411-043: Under a boost of the target along $x^-$ direction with parameter $\gamma_t$, a current associated with the target (which can be color, flavor or baryon number for example) scale as \begin{equation}\label{eq:current_scaling} J^-(x)\propto \gamma_t \, , \:\: J^j(x)\propto (\gamma_t)^0\, , \:\: J^+(x)\propto (\gamma_t)^{-1}\end{equation} In order to understand the scaling behaviour of the quark background field $\Psi(x)$ of the target, it convenient to introduce the projections \begin{equation}\Psi^{(-)}(x)\equiv \frac{\gamma^+\gamma^-}{2}\Psi(x) \Psi^{(+)}(x)\equiv \frac{\gamma^-\gamma^+}{2}\Psi(x)\end{equation} which are known to be good and bad components of $\Psi(x)$ respectively, for a left-moving target.
elements.sent-2411-044: The currents constructed as bilinears of $\Psi(x)$ have components that depends on the good and bad components introduce above which read \begin{equation}\label{eq:projected_minus} \overline{\Psi}(x)\gamma^-\Psi(x) =\overline{\Psi^{(-)}}(x)\gamma^-\Psi^{(-)}(x) \overline{\Psi}(x)\gamma^j\Psi(x) =\overline{\Psi^{(-)}}(x)\gamma^j\Psi^{(+)}(x) + \overline{\Psi^{(+)}}(x)\gamma^j\Psi^{(-)}(x) \overline{\Psi}(x)\gamma^+\Psi(x) =\overline{\Psi^{(+)}}(x)\gamma^-\Psi^{(+)}(x)\end{equation} Such currents associated with the target have to follow the same scaling behaviour introduced in Eq. \eqref{eq:current_scaling , so that the components quark background field scale as \begin{equation}\Psi^{(-)}(x)\propto (\gamma_t)^{\frac{1}{2}} \nn \Psi^{(+)}(x)\propto (\gamma_t)^{-\frac{1}{2}}\end{equation} under a large boost of the target with $\gamma_t$.}
elements.sent-2411-045: The quark background field does not contribute at eikonal order, and thanks to these scaling properties, only the enhanced components $\Psi^{(-) (x)$ can contribute to NEik corrections, whereas the suppressed components $\Psi^{(+) (x)$ start to contribute only at next-to-next-to-eikonal (NNEik) accuracy.}}
elements.sent-2411-046: The studies to include NEik corrections in the CGC calculations have been quite advanced over the last decade.
elements.sent-2411-047: The first studies accounting for the finite longitudinal width of the target in the gluon propagator are performed in \cite{Altinoluk:2014oxa at NEik accuracy and in \cite{Altinoluk:2015gia at NNEik accuracy.}}
elements.sent-2411-048: The results of these studies are used to compute many different observables beyond eikonal approximation.
elements.sent-2411-049: The effects of NEik corrections on particle production and correlations are studied both for dilute-dilute \cite{Altinoluk:2015xuy,Agostini:2019avp,Agostini:2019hkj and in dilute-dense \cite{Agostini:2022ctk,Agostini:2022oge collisions.}}
elements.sent-2411-050: The NEik corrections to the quark propagator that stems from the finite-longitudinal width of target and the interactions with the transverse component of the background field are studied in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd .}
elements.sent-2411-051: A similar study is performed for the scalar propagator in \cite{Agostini:2023cvc .}
elements.sent-2411-052: In \cite{Altinoluk:2021lvu , NEik corrections that are originating from dynamics of the target by going beyond the static limit of the target fields are studied both for scalar and quark propagators.}
elements.sent-2411-053: The results are used to compute the DIS dijet production at full NEik accuracy in a dynamical gluon background both for a dense target in \cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk and for a dilute target in \cite{Agostini:2024xqs .}}
elements.sent-2411-054: On the other hand, the NEik effects stemming from including the interaction of the projectile partons with quark background field via t-channel quark exchanges are studied to compute quark-gluon dijet production in DIS in \cite{Altinoluk:2023qfr .}
elements.sent-2411-055: In this study, the back-to-back limit of the produced jets is considered in order to probe the quark TMDs starting from CGC calculations with NEik corrections.
elements.sent-2411-056: Last but not least, the back-to-back limit of the DIS dijet production at NEik order is studied in \cite{Altinoluk:2024zom in order to probe various different gluon TMDs beyond the eikonal and the leading twist approximations.}
elements.sent-2411-057: Apart from the above mentioned works that focus on the derivation of the NEik corrections to parton propagators and their applications to various scattering processes, in \cite{Kovchegov:2015pbl,Kovchegov:2016zex,Kovchegov:2016weo,Kovchegov:2017jxc,Kovchegov:2017lsr,Kovchegov:2018znm,Kovchegov:2018zeq,Kovchegov:2020kxg,Kovchegov:2020hgb,Adamiak:2021ppq,Kovchegov:2021lvz,Kovchegov:2021iyc,Cougoulic:2022gbk,Kovchegov:2022kyy,Borden:2023ugd,Kovchegov:2024aus quark and gluon helicity evolutions as well as observables such as single and/or double spin asymmetries are computed at NEik accuracy.}
elements.sent-2411-058: In \cite{Cougoulic:2019aja,Cougoulic:2020tbc , helicity dependent extensions of the CGC have been studied at NEik accuracy.}
elements.sent-2411-059: In \cite{Balitsky:2015qba,Balitsky:2016dgz,Balitsky:2017flc , the rapidity evolution of transverse momentum dependent parton distributions (TMDs) have been computed in a way that would be valid both at moderate and at low values of the momentum fraction $x$.}
elements.sent-2411-060: A similar idea is pursued to study the interpolation between the moderate and low values of $x$ both for inclusive DIS \cite{Boussarie:2020fpb,Boussarie:2021wkn and also for exclusive Compton scattering processes \cite{Boussarie:2023xun .}}
elements.sent-2411-061: In \cite{Chirilli:2018kkw,Chirilli:2021lif , the subeikonal corrections to both quark and gluon propagators are computed in high-energy operator product expansion (OPE) formalism.}
elements.sent-2411-062: Subeikonal corrections in the CGC are studied in an effective Hamiltonian approach in \cite{Li:2023tlw,Li:2024fdb,Li:2024xra .}
elements.sent-2411-063: Finally, subeikonal corrections are also investigated in \cite{Jalilian-Marian:2017ttv,Jalilian-Marian:2018iui,Jalilian-Marian:2019kaf by introducing an approach that allows longitudinal momentum exchange between the projectile and the target during the interaction.}
elements.sent-2411-064: Last but not least, the effects of subeikonal corrections are also studied in the context of orbital angular momentum \cite{Hatta:2016aoc,Kovchegov:2019rrz,Boussarie:2019icw, Kovchegov:2023yzd, Kovchegov:2024wjs .}
elements.sent-2411-065: In this paper, we present a comprehensive study of both quark and gluon propagators in a dynamical gluon background field at NEik accuracy, and compute various parton production cross sections in parton-nucleus scatterings at forward rapidity at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-066: The outline of the paper is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-067: In Section \ref{Sec:before-to-after-gluon_prop , we derive the before-to-after gluon propagator in the presence of dynamical gluon background field at NEik accuracy.}
elements.sent-2411-068: In section \ref{sec:Various_parton_prop , we present the expressions of various parton propagators with their starting positions either before or inside the medium and final positions either inside or after the medium.}
elements.sent-2411-069: Section \ref{sec:gluon_production_in_gA is devoted to the computation of the forward gluon production cross section in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy.}
elements.sent-2411-070: Similarly, Section \ref{sec:quark_in_quark-nucleus is devoted to the computation of the forward quark production cross section in quark-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy.}
elements.sent-2411-071: Sections \ref{sec:g_prod_in_qA and \ref{sec:q_prod_in_gA are presenting further applications of the computed propagators.}}
elements.sent-2411-072: In Section \ref{sec:g_prod_in_qA , the forward gluon production cross section in quark-nucleus scattering and in Section \ref{sec:q_prod_in_gA the forward quark production cross section in gluon-nucleus scattering are computed at NEik accuracy.}}
elements.sent-2411-073: Finally, in Section \ref{sec:outlook we summarize our results and present an outlook.}
elements.sent-2411-074: Moreover, in Appendix \ref{app:derivation_NEik_pure_A- , we provide the details of the derivation of the NEik corrections beyond the shockwave approximation for the before-to-after gluon propagator.}
elements.sent-2411-075: Appendix \ref{App:inside-to-inside-quark is devoted to the presentation of the derivation of the inside-to-inside quark propagator.}
elements.sent-2411-076: Finally, in Appendix \ref{App:inside-to-after-gluon-prop we present the derivation of the inside-to-after gluon propagator.}

elements.subsection-2411-002: NEik gluon propagator in a gluon background field
elements.sent-2411-132: We are now ready to discuss the gluon propagator at full NEik accuracy in a dynamical gluon background field.
elements.sent-2411-133: There are three types of NEik corrections in this context: NEik corrections beyond the static approximation (that are already included in Eq.~\eqref{EikF ), NEik corrections beyond the shockwave approximation (corresponding to the difference between Eqs.~\eqref{Without shockwave approx G and \eqref{With shockwave approx G ), and NEik corrections associated with insertions of the transverse components of the background field.}}}
elements.sent-2411-134: At NEik accuracy, the gluon propagator get contributions from interactions with the "$-$" component of the background field but including the corrections stemming from finite longitudinal extent.
elements.sent-2411-135: Moreover, it also receives contributions from the interactions with the transverse component of the background field.

elements.subsubsection-2411-001: NEik gluon propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background
elements.sent-2411-140: The derivation of NEik corrections in pure $\cal A^-$ background have been discussed in detail for gluon propagator in \cite{Altinoluk:2014oxa and for quark propagator in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd, Altinoluk:2021lvu .}}
elements.sent-2411-141: Here, we follow the same strategy adopted in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd to compute the medium corrections to a gluon propagator at NEik accuracy in a pure $\cal A^-$ background passing through the whole medium.}
elements.sent-2411-142: Since the derivation is almost the same as in the case of quark propagator, here we only present the result (more details on the derivation can be found in Appendix \ref{app:derivation_NEik_pure_A- ).}

elements.section-2411-003: Various parton propagators in a gluon background field
elements.sent-2411-189: This section is devoted to provide the expressions for both quark and gluon propagators with either their starting position or final position inside the medium.
elements.sent-2411-190: In the calculation of scattering processes at NEik accuracy, the expression of the propagators in these special configurations are typically needed only at eikonal (or generalized eikonal) order, and we will thus restrict ourselves to that precision in this section.
elements.sent-2411-191: Some of those propagators have been computed in our earlier works \cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk,Altinoluk:2023qfr .}
elements.sent-2411-192: The details of the derivation of the new propagators are provided in Appendices \ref{App:inside-to-inside-quark and \ref{App:inside-to-after-gluon-prop while their results are given in this section.}}
elements.sent-2411-193: The quark propagator from inside to after the medium, corresponding to $x^+>L^+/2$ and $-L^+/2<y^+<L^+/2$, is computed at generalized eikonal order in Ref.~\cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk and it reads \begin{equation}\label{q IA} S_{F}(x,y) \big|^{\rm IA,q}_{\rm Gen. Eik} = \int\! \frac{d^{3}\uq}{(2\pi)^{3}} \frac{\theta(q^{+})}{2q^{+}}\ e^{-ix\check{q}} \ (\check{\sq}+m) \ U_{F}(x^{+},y^{+};\y, y^{-}) \bigg[1-\frac{\gamma^{+}\gamma^{i}}{2q^{+}}i\overleftarrow{D}^{F}_{\y^i}\bigg] \ e^{iy^{-}q^{+}} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\q}\end{equation} which in the strict eikonal limit can be written as \begin{equation}\label{q_IA_Eik} S_{F}(x,y) \big|^{\rm IA,q}_{\rm Eik} = \int\! \frac{d^{3}q}{(2\pi)^{3}} \frac{\theta(q^{+})}{2q^{+}}\ e^{-ix\check{q}} \ (\check{\sq}+m) \ U_{F}(x^{+},y^{+};\y) \bigg[1-\frac{\gamma^{+}\gamma^{i}}{2q^{+}}i\overleftarrow{D}^{F}_{\y^i}\bigg] \ e^{iy^{-}q^{+}} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\q} \, .\end{equation}.}

elements.section-2411-004: Forward gluon production in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy
elements.sent-2411-212: So far we have computed various parton propagators relevant for scattering processes at NEik accuracy in Sec.
elements.sent-2411-213: This section is devoted to an application of those results to forward single inclusive gluon production in gluon-nucleus collisions at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-214: The cross sections that we consider in the rest of this manuscript are partonic cross sections.
elements.sent-2411-215: In general, in order to be able to compare with the experimental data one should consider the hadronic cross sections.
elements.sent-2411-216: This would require convolution of the computed partonic cross section with the relevant parton distribution functions (PDFs) and fragmentation functions (FFs).
elements.sent-2411-217: Our goal in this manuscript is to provide the partonic cross sections at full NEik accuracy for the first time and since we do not intend to provide numerical comparison of our results with the experimental data, we only consider the partonic cross sections.
elements.sent-2411-218: Moreover, the analysis and discussion of the NEik corrections to cross sections are independent of the convolution of the partonic results with PDFs and FFs.
elements.sent-2411-219: This step, together with the numerical comparison of our results with the experimental data, is left for future studies since models to describe the operators that are obtained at NEik order are not available yet.
elements.sent-2411-220: At partonic level, forward gluon production in gluon-nucleus scattering gets two separate contributions at NEik accuracy. \begin{equation}\label{eq:gAtogX_gen} \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.} = \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{g \, \rm backg.} + \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{q \, \rm backg.}\end{equation} As discussed in detail in Sec.
elements.formula-2411-070: \begin{equation}\label{eq:gAtogX_gen} \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.} = \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{g \, \rm backg.} + \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{q \, \rm backg.}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-221: For the second contribution in Eq. \eqref{eq:gAtogX_gen , a quark background field for the target is turned on, on top of the gluon background field, and interactions of the propagating gluon with that quark background field are calculated at NEik order, accounting for scattering via t-channel quark exchange with the target.}
elements.sent-2411-222: In the rest of this section, we compute each term separately and finally give the total result.
elements.subsection-2411-010: Forward gluon production cross section in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy: pure gluon background contribution

elements.sent-2411-229: Our aim in this subsection is to compute the gluon background contribution to the gluon production cross section beyond the static limit of the target, i.e. we would like to keep the $z^-$ dependence of the gluon background field.
elements.sent-2411-230: The computation of a cross section beyond the static limit of the target fields is discussed in detail in \cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk .}
elements.sent-2411-231: Here, we follow the same method.
elements.sent-2411-232: For that purpose, we first get the $z^-$ dependent scattering amplitude from the S-matrix element, which reads \begin{equation}\label{Scattering amplitude gluon on gluon} i {\cal M}_{ab}^{\lambda_{1} \lambda_{2}}(z^{-}, k, q) = \int \! d^{2}\z\ e^{-iz\cdot(\q -\k)} \Bigg\{ {\epsilon_{\lambda_{2}}^{i}}^{*} \ \epsilon_{\lambda_{1}}^{i} \bigg[\frac{(2q^{+})}{(q^{+}+k^{+})} \ U_{A}(\z,z^{-}) - \frac{(\q^{j}+ \k^{j})}{4k^{+}} U_{A;j}^{(1)}(\z,z^{-}) -\frac{i}{2k^{+}} \ U_{A}^{(2)}(\z,z^{-}) \bigg] \nn -\frac{2}{2k^{+}} {\epsilon_{\lambda_{2}}^{i}}^{*} \ \epsilon_{\lambda_{1}}^{j} \ U_{R;ij}^{(3)}(\z,z^{-}) \Bigg\} + NNEik\end{equation} From the $z^-$ dependent scattering amplitude, one can compute the cross section as\footnote{For interested reader, we refer to Appendix B of \cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk for a detailed discussion of how to get the cross section from the scattering amplitude in the case of $z^-$ dependent gluon background field. \begin{equation}\label{Definition of cross section z- dependent for gluon} {\frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}} = 2k^{+} \int\! dr^{-} \ e^{ir^{-}(q^{+}-k^{+})} \frac{1}{2(N_c^2 -1)} \sum_{\lambda_1, \lambda_2} \sum_{a,b} \Big\langle {M_{ab}^{\lambda_1 \lambda_2}\Big(\frac{-r^{-}}{2}, k, q\Big)^{\dagger}\ M_{ab}^{\lambda_1 \lambda_2}\Big(\frac{r^{-}}{2}, k, q\Big)}\Big\rangle\end{equation} where the one-particle phase space is defined as \begin{equation}d{\rm P.S}. = \frac{1}{2q^+}\frac{dq^{+}}{(2\pi)} \frac{d^{2} \q}{(2\pi)^2} \: .\end{equation}.}}

elements.subsection-2411-011: Forward gluon production cross section in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy: quark background contribution
elements.sent-2411-245: At NEik accuracy, the other contribution to the forward gluon production cross section in gluon-nucleus scattering originates from the interaction with the quark background field.
elements.sent-2411-246: This corresponds to the second term in Eq. \eqref{eq:gAtogX_gen .}
elements.sent-2411-247: This section is devoted to the computation of this contribution.
elements.sent-2411-248: The quark background contribution to the gluon production in gluon-nucleus scattering can be considered as two separate sub contributions which can be written as \begin{equation}\label{eq:q_backg_total_gen} \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{q\, \rm backg.}= \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{gqg}+\frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{g{\bar q}g} \: .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-080: \begin{equation}\label{eq:q_backg_total_gen} \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{q\, \rm backg.}= \frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{gqg}+\frac{d \sigma^{gA\rightarrow g+X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{g{\bar q}g} \: .\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-249: The first term in Eq. \eqref{eq:q_backg_total_gen corresponds to the case where the incoming gluon interacts with a quark background field converting the gluon into a quark which later interacts with antiquark field converting the quark back into a gluon before it goes out of the medium (see the left panel of Fig. \ref{fig:gluon in q BF case 1 q ).}}
elements.sent-2411-250: On the other hand, the second term in Eq. \eqref{eq:q_backg_total_gen corresponds to the case where the incoming gluon interacts with an antiquark background field converting the gluon into an antiquark that later interacts with a quark background field converting it back into a gluon and finally gluon exceeds the medium (see the right panel of Fig. \ref{fig:gluon in q BF case 1 q )\footnote{In principle, there is a third contribution, with the instantaneous term from the quark propagator between the two quark background field insertions.}}}
elements.sent-2411-251: However, in that contribution, the enhanced components of the quark background field are projected out, and only the suppressed component survive, giving a contribution to the process only at NNEik order.

elements.sent-2411-293: Therefore, when computing the quark background contribution to the forward quark production cross section in quark-nucleus scattering, one should only account for the interference of quark background contribution to the quark production amplitude and the Eikonal piece of the quark production amplitude in a gluon background field to stay at NEik accuracy at the level of the cross section.
elements.sent-2411-294: The quark scattering amplitude in a gluon background field at Eikonal order is very well known and it was also computed in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd as \begin{equation}\label{Scattering amp q eik} iM^{h'h}_{\beta\alpha}(z^{-}, k, q)\big|^{\rm Eik}_{g \, \rm backg.} = \frac{1}{2k^{+}} \int\! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\q-\k)} \ \overline{u}(\check{q},h') \ U_{F} ( \z,z^{-})_{\beta\alpha} \ \gamma^{+} \ u(\check{k},h)\end{equation} Schematically, the quark contribution to the $z^-$ dependent quark production cross section can be written as \cite{Altinoluk:2022jkk \begin{equation}\label{eq:q_background_1} \frac{d\sigma^{qA\rightarrow q+ X}}{d\rm P.S.}\bigg|_{q\, \rm backg.} = 2k^{+} \int\! dr^{-} \ e^{ir^{-}(q^{+}-k^{+})} \sum_{h,h'}\sum_{\alpha, \beta} \Big[ \Big\langle {M_{F}\Big(\frac{-r}{2},k, q\Big)^{\dagger}\Big|_{\rm Eik} M_{F}\Big(\frac{r^{-}}{2}, k, q\Big)\Big|_{\rm NEik}} \Big\rangle \nn + \Big\langle {M_{F}\Big(\frac{-r}{2},k, q\Big)^{\dagger}\Big|_{\rm NEik} M_{F}\Big(\frac{r^{-}}{2}, k, q\Big)\Big|_{\rm Eik}} \Big\rangle\Big]\end{equation} where the NEik amplitude is the quark production amplitude in quark background field given in Eq. \eqref{Scattering amp qgq neik and the Eikonal amplitude is the quark production amplitude in gluon background field given in Eq. \eqref{Scattering amp q eik .}}}}
elements.formula-2411-106: \begin{equation}\label{Scattering amp q eik} iM^{h'h}_{\beta\alpha}(z^{-}, k, q)\big|^{\rm Eik}_{g \, \rm backg.} = \frac{1}{2k^{+}} \int\! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\q-\k)} \ \overline{u}(\check{q},h') \ U_{F} ( \z,z^{-})_{\beta\alpha} \ \gamma^{+} \ u(\check{k},h)\end{equation}

elements.section-2411-006: Forward gluon production in quark-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy
elements.sent-2411-300: Apart from the forward gluon production in gluon-nucleus scattering (Sec.~\ref{sec:gluon_production_in_gA ) and the forward quark production in quark-nucleus scattering (Sec.~\ref{sec:quark_in_quark-nucleus ), at NEik accuracy one actually has two more mechanism for gluon or quark production.}}
elements.sent-2411-301: The first one is the forward gluon production in quark nucleus scattering and the second one is the forward quark production in gluon-nucleus scattering.
elements.sent-2411-302: Both of these two mechanisms originate from quark background scattering in the amplitude and in the complex conjugate amplitude, so that the obtained cross section is at NEik order.
elements.sent-2411-303: Let us start with the computation of the forward gluon production cross section in quark-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-304: In this mechanism, the incoming quark interact with antiquark background field and it converts into a gluon both in the amplitude and in the complex conjugate amplitude.
elements.sent-2411-305: The mixed propagator for this case simply reads.
elements.figure-2411-007: \includegraphics[width=0.5\linewidth]{q_to_g_casei.pdf} \caption{Diagram for quark to gluon conversion due to the quark background field of the target} \label{fig: case 1}
elements.sent-2411-306: \begin{equation}\label{General case 1} M^{\mu}_{a,\alpha}(x,y) \big|_{q\, {\rm to}\, g } = \int \! d^{4}z \ G^{\mu \mu'}_{F}(x,z)_{ab}\big|^{\rm IA}_{\rm Eik} \ \overline{\Psi}^{-}_{\beta}(z) \ \Big[ (-igt^{b} \gamma_{\mu'}) \ S_{F}(z,y)\big|^{\rm BI}_{\rm Eik}\Big]_{\beta \alpha}\end{equation} Substituting the explicit expressions of before-to-inside quark propagator Eq. \eqref{BI quark and inside-to-after gluon propagator Eq. \eqref{eq:inside-after_gluon into the mixed propagator and using the following relations for the Dirac matrices \begin{equation}\gamma^{0}\gamma^{-}\gamma^{+} \gamma_{\mu'} \gamma^{+} \gamma^{i} = \gamma^{0}\gamma^{-} \big( \{\gamma^{+}, \gamma_{\mu'}\} - \gamma_{\mu'} \gamma^{+}\big)\gamma^{+} \gamma^{i} \nn = \gamma^{0}\gamma^{-}\gamma^{+} \gamma^{i}\big( 2g^{+}_{\mu'}\big)\end{equation} to simplify the resulting expression, one obtains \begin{equation}\label{eq:prop_q_to_g} M^{\mu}_{a,\alpha}(x,y)\big|_{q\, {\rm to}\,g} = \int \! \frac{d^{3}\uq}{(2\pi)^{3}} \frac{\theta(q^{+})}{2q^+} \ \int \! \frac{d^{3}\uk}{(2 \pi)^3} \ \frac{ \theta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-ix \cdot \check{q}} \ e^{iy \cdot \check{k}} \int \! d^4z \ e^{iz^-(q^+-k^{+})} \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\q-\k)} \ U_{A}(x^{+},z^{+},\z,z^{-})_{ab} \nn \times \Big[g^{\mu}_{j} + \frac{\eta^{\mu} \q^{j}}{q^{+}}\Big] \ \overline{\Psi}_{\beta}(z) \Big[\ \frac{\gamma^{-}\gamma^{+} \gamma^{j}}{2}\Big] \ (\check{\sk} + m) \ \Big[\big(igt^{b}\big) \ U_{F}(z^+,y^+,\z,z^{-})\Big]_{\beta \alpha}\end{equation} We can now use the obtained mixed propagator to compute the S-matrix element.}}

elements.section-2411-007: Forward quark production in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy
elements.sent-2411-311: The last process that we are interested in studying is the forward quark production in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-312: In this process, the incoming gluon interacts with the quark background field of the target, converts into a quark and this quark exits the target medium (see Fig. \ref{fig: case 2 ).}
elements.sent-2411-313: The computation of the forward quark production in gluon-nucleus scattering at NEik accuracy closely follow the forward gluon production in quark-nucleus scattering studied in Sec.. \ref{sec:g_prod_in_qA .}
elements.sent-2411-314: As in the previous case, we start with the mixed propagator for this case which reads.

elements.sent-2411-320: In this paper, we presented a comprehensive study of both gluon and quark propagators at full NEik accuracy in a dynamical gluon background field, and included the effects of a quark background field at NEik accuracy as well.
elements.sent-2411-321: We start with the derivation of the before-to-after gluon propagator in a dynamical gluon propagator at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-322: The NEik corrections to before-to-after gluon propagator stemming from relaxing the shockwave approximation were first computed in \cite{Altinoluk:2014oxa .}
elements.sent-2411-323: In this paper, we perform the derivation of those corrections in a more systematic way.
elements.sent-2411-324: Moreover, we also derive the NEik corrections beyond the static target field limit as well as the corrections originating from the interaction with the transverse component of the background gluon field.
elements.sent-2411-325: On the other hand, before-to-after quark propagator at NEik accuracy was computed in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd,Altinoluk:2021lvu .}
elements.sent-2411-326: Therefore, in this paper we just present those results for completeness.
elements.sent-2411-327: Apart from before-to-after quark and gluon propagators, in this work we perform the derivations of before-to-inside, inside-to-inside and inside-to-after quark and gluon propagators at eikonal accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-328: Even though these propagators are computed at eikonal order, they typically contribute only at NEik order to observables since either one or both legs of these propagators are inside the medium.
elements.sent-2411-329: Finally, by using all these propagators, we compute single inclusive quark and single inclusive gluon forward production cross sections in quark-nucleus and gluon-nucleus scatterings at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-330: In the computation of gluon production in gluon-nucleus scattering and quark production in quark-nucleus scattering both gluon background and quark background contributions are taken into account.
elements.sent-2411-331: On the other hand, in the computation of gluon production in quark-nucleus scattering and quark production in gluon-nucleus scattering pure gluon background does not contribute since one needs at least one t-channel quark exchange in order to convert an incoming quark into a gluon or an incoming gluon into a quark.
elements.sent-2411-332: Before-to-after, before-to-inside, inside-to-inside and inside-to-after quark and gluon propagators computed in this paper can serve as building blocks for computing many observables at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-333: As a natural continuation of this work and as an immediate application of the results derived here, we are planing to study forward dijet production in pA collisions at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-334: It would be interesting to understand the interplay between the kinematic twist corrections and the NEik corrections in the back-to-back limit in that context.
elements.sent-2411-335: Another interesting observable that can be computed by using the propagators derived in this paper is single inclusive jet production in deep inelastic scattering (SIDIS) at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-336: As recently shown in \cite{Altinoluk:2024vgg , in the strict eikonal limit, CGC computations only provide gluon-splitting contribution to the quark TMD.}
elements.sent-2411-337: However, at NEik accuracy, by including a t-channel quark exchange, one may achieve to a complete connection with quark TMD from the CGC calculations.
elements.sent-2411-338: Last but not least, one can also study the back-to-back limit of photon+jet production at forward rapidity in pA collisions at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-339: At eikonal order, this observable is known to probe dipole gluon TMD without taking the back-to-back limit since photon does not interact with the target \cite{Dominguez:2011wm .}
elements.sent-2411-340: However, it is not clear whether this remains true at NEik accuracy.
elements.sent-2411-341: Therefore, one should compute the photon+jet production at NEik order in a pure gluon background to see if this statement holds and what type of corrections one gets to the gluon TMD at NEik order.
elements.sent-2411-342: Moreover, by including a t-channel quark exchange in the photon+jet production at NEik order, one can also get access to quark TMDs which we are planing include in our future study.

elements.section-2411-009: Derivation of the NEik corrections to the gluon propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background
elements.sent-2411-344: This section is devoted to the details of the derivation of the medium corrections to the gluon propagator at NEik accuracy in gluon $\A^-$ background field, in particular NEik contributions beyond the shockwave limit.
elements.sent-2411-345: The steps in this derivation is closely related with the derivation of NEik corrections to a quark propagator performed in \cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd .}
elements.sent-2411-346: The new feature in the derivation of the medium corrections to the gluon propagator at NEik accuracy presented in the rest of this section is the inclusion of NEik corrections beyond the static approximation of the target, by keeping an overall $z^-$ dependence of the background field.
elements.sent-2411-347: The medium corrections to a gluon propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background before performing the shockwave approximation is given in Eq. \eqref{Without shockwave approx G , up to NNEik corrections beyond the static limit.}
elements.sent-2411-349: The idea of the shockwave approximation, which is part of the Eikonal approximation, is that in the limit of large boost of the target, the target becomes infinitely Lorentz contracted along the $x^+$ direction, so that partons scattering on that target have no time (along $x^+$) to have a noticeable transverse motion while they cross the whole target.
elements.sent-2411-350: Hence, in that limit, all of the interactions with the background field along the same propagator happens at the same transverse position $\z$, see Eq.~\eqref{delta_G_bef_Wilson .}
elements.sent-2411-351: The NEik corrections beyond the shockwave limit stem from the difference between the true transverse positions of the interaction vertices with the background field and the approximate position $\z$.
elements.sent-2411-352: For that calculation, we follow the same steps as in Ref.~\cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd , where the case of the quark propagator was considered.}
elements.sent-2411-353: By comparison, one can realize that for $k^+=q^+$ the last two lines of Eq. \eqref{eq:delta_G_gen_1 are the same as the last three lines of Eq. (A1) of Ref.~\cite{Altinoluk:2020oyd for the case of $x^+>y^+$, up the change of color representation.}}
elements.sent-2411-354: Hence the same procedure can be followed to collect the field insertions to write the result in terms of Wilson lines.

elements.sent-2411-360: As it we will explain in detail in this appendix, the inside-to-inside quark propagator at eikonal order gets contribution from the interaction of with $\A^-$ and $\A^j$ components of the background gluon field.
elements.subsection-2411-016: Inside-to-inside quark propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background at eikonal order
elements.formula-2411-145: \begin{equation}\label{eq:II_quark_A_minus_1} S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A^{-}} = \int \! \frac{d^{3}\up}{(2 \pi)^3} \ \int \! \frac{d^{3}\uk}{(2 \pi)^3}\ e^{-ix \cdot \check{p} + iy \cdot \check{k}} \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(p^+ - k^+)} \ \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \frac{\check{ \sp}+ m}{2p^+} \gamma^{+} \nn \times \theta(p^+) \theta(k^+) \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z,z^-) \frac{(\check{\sk} + m)}{2k^+}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-362: In that regime, $x^+$ and $y^+$ are of order $L^+$ which is power suppressed in the shockwave limit.
elements.sent-2411-363: Hence, at eikonal order, it is safe to neglect all terms proportional to $x^+$ and $y^+$ in the phases.
elements.sent-2411-364: Moreover, one can also expand the gamma matrix structure term by term to simplify it.
elements.sent-2411-365:
Using the relation,  egin{equation}\gamma^+ \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^- = \Big[ \{\gamma^+, \gamma^-\} - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \Big] \gamma^+ \gamma^- = 2g^{+-} \gamma^+ \gamma^- = 2 \gamma^+ \gamma^-\end{equation} one obtains  egin{equation}\label{eq:gamma_Struc_II_A_minus} (\check{\slashed{p}} + m) \gamma^+ (\check{ \slashed{k} } + m) = (\gamma^+ \check{p}^- + \gamma^- p^+ - \gamma^i \p^i + m) \gamma^+ (\gamma^+ \check{k}^- + \gamma^- k^+ - \gamma^j \k^j + m) 
n = 2\gamma^- p^+ k^+ - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j p^+ \k^j + \gamma^- \gamma^+ m p^+ - \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i k^+ 
n + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j - \gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i + \gamma^+ \gamma^- m k^+ - \gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j + m^2 \gamma^+ 
n = 2\gamma^- p^+ k^+ - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j p^+ \k^j + \gamma^- \gamma^+ m p^+ + \gamma^+ \gamma^- m k^+ - \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i k^+ 
n + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j - \gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i - \gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j + m^2 \gamma^+\end{equation} Substituting the simplified form of the gamma matrix structure given in Eq. \eqref{eq:gamma_Struc_II_A_minus into Eq. \eqref{eq:II_quark_A_minus_1 and approximating the phase factor in $y^+$ and $x^+$ by one, we get  egin{equation}S_{F}(x,y)  ig|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{II,q} = \int \!  rac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \!  rac{dp^+}{2\pi} \int \!  rac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2}\ \int \!  rac{dk^+}{2\pi}  rac{ 	heta(p^+)}{2p^+}  rac{ 	heta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-ix^-p^+} e^{i\x\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^{-}k^{+}} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k} 
n 	imes \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(p^+ - k^+)} \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \Big[ 2\gamma^- p^+ k^+ - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j p^+ \k^j - \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i k^+ 
n + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j +\gamma^- \gamma^+ m p^+ + \gamma^+ \gamma^- m k^+ - \gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i - \gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j + m^2 \gamma^+ \Big] 
n 	imes U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z,z^-)\end{equation} Since we are interested in calculating the propagator at Eikonal order, we can perform a Taylor expansion of the Wilson line around an initial value of the minus coordinate $z^-=x^-$ and take the zeroth order term in the expansion.}}
elements.formula-2411-147:
egin{equation}\label{eq:gamma_Struc_II_A_minus} (\check{\slashed{p}} + m) \gamma^+ (\check{ \slashed{k} } + m) = (\gamma^+ \check{p}^- + \gamma^- p^+ - \gamma^i \p^i + m) \gamma^+ (\gamma^+ \check{k}^- + \gamma^- k^+ - \gamma^j \k^j + m) 
n = 2\gamma^- p^+ k^+ - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j p^+ \k^j + \gamma^- \gamma^+ m p^+ - \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i k^+ 
n + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j - \gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i + \gamma^+ \gamma^- m k^+ - \gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j + m^2 \gamma^+ 
n = 2\gamma^- p^+ k^+ - \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j p^+ \k^j + \gamma^- \gamma^+ m p^+ + \gamma^+ \gamma^- m k^+ - \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i k^+ 
n + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j - \gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i - \gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j + m^2 \gamma^+\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-150: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2}\ \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \theta(k^+) \ e^{-ix^-k^+} e^{i\x\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^{-}k^{+}} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k} \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \Big[ \frac{\gamma^-}{2} - \frac{1}{4k^+}(\gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j \k^j + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \p^i ) + \frac{\gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j \p^i \k^j}{4(k^+)^2} \nn + \frac{m}{2k^+} - \frac{\gamma^i \gamma^+ m \p^i}{4(k^+)^2} - \frac{\gamma^+ \gamma^j m \k^j}{4(k^+)^2} + \frac{m^2 \gamma^+}{4(k^+)^2} \Big] U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-151: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \theta(k^+) \ e^{-ix^-k^+} \ e^{i\x\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^{-}k^{+}} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k} \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \bigg[ \frac{\gamma^-}{2} + \frac{m}{2k^+}+ \frac{m^2 \gamma^+}{4(k^+)^2} - \frac{1}{4k^+}\Big[ \gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j \Big(i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\y^j}}\Big) + \gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^- \Big(-i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\x^i}}\Big) \Big] \nn + \frac{\gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j }{4(k^+)^2} \Big(-i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\x^i}}\Big) \Big(i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\y^j}}\Big) - \frac{\gamma^i \gamma^+ m }{4(k^+)^2} \Big(-i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\x^i}}\Big) - \frac{\gamma^+ \gamma^j m }{4(k^+)^2} \Big(i \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\y^j}}\Big) \bigg] U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation}
elements.subsection-2411-017: Eikonal contribution to inside-to-inside quark propagator from $\A_{\perp}
elements.sent-2411-368: At eikonal order, the integration over the longitudinal coordinate gives a factor of $L^+$ which is of order $1/\gamma_t$ and it comes with $\A^-$ that is order $\gamma_t$, thus giving an order $(\gamma_t)^0$ contribution over all.
elements.sent-2411-369: Similarly, one can consider the instantaneous part of the quark propagator which gives an order $(\gamma_t)^0$ contribution when integrated over the longitudinal coordinate.
elements.sent-2411-370: If the one considers the insertion of a transverse component of the background field that is of order $(\gamma_t)^0$ coupled to the instantaneous part of the quark propagator, one gets an overall order $(\gamma_t)^0$ term which is an eikonal contribution.
elements.sent-2411-371: There are three such contributions that would give terms at Eikonal order.
elements.sent-2411-372: First contribution comes from inserting a transverse background field on the side of $x^+$.
elements.sent-2411-373: In this case, the propagator can be written as \begin{equation}\label{eq:contr_x+side} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ S_{F}(x,w)\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{Inst.\, q} \ (-ig \gamma^j t^a) A^{a}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q}\end{equation} where we have an eikonal quark propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background from point $y$ to $w$.
elements.formula-2411-153: \begin{equation}\label{eq:contr_x+side} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ S_{F}(x,w)\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{Inst.\, q} \ (-ig \gamma^j t^a) A^{a}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-374: At point $w$, we insert the transverse background field and from point $w$ to $x$ we have the instantaneous quark propagator.
elements.formula-2411-154: \begin{equation}\label{eq:contr_x+side_2} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp}, x}^{II,q} = \int \! d^4 w \delta^{3}(x - w) \gamma^+ \ \int \! \frac{dq^+}{2 \pi} \frac{i}{2q^+} \ e^{-iq^{+}(x^{-} - w^{-})} \Big[ (-ig \gamma^j t^a) \A^{a}_{j}(w) \Big] \nn \times \int \! \frac{d^{3}\up}{(2 \pi)^3} \ \int \! \frac{d^{3}\uk}{(2 \pi)^3} \ e^{-iw \cdot \check{p} + iy \cdot \check{k}} \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(p^+ - k^+)} \ \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \frac{(\check{\slashed{p}}+ m)}{2p^+} \gamma^{+} \nn \times \theta(p^+) \theta(k^+) \ U_{F}(w^+,y^+,\z,z^-) \frac{(\check{\slashed{k}} + m)}{2k^+}\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-156: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^4 w \ \delta^{2}(\x - \w) \delta(x^+ - w^+) \ \int \! \frac{dq^+}{2 \pi} \frac{i}{2q^+} \ e^{-iq^{+}(x^{-} - w^{-})} \big[ig t^a \ A^{a}_{j}(w)\big] \nn \times \int \! \frac{dp^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \ e^{-iw^- p^+} \ e^{i \w\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^-k^+} \ e^{-i\y\cdot \k} \ \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(p^+ - k^+)} \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z(\p - \k)} \ \theta(p^+) \theta(k^+) \bigg[ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \Big( \frac{\gamma^-}{2} - \frac{\gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} + \frac{m}{2k^+}\Big) \bigg] U_{F}(w^+,y^+,\z,z^-)\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-157: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! dw^- \ \int \! \frac{dq^+}{2 \pi} \frac{i}{2q^+} \int \! \frac{dp^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \nn \times e^{-iq^{+}x^{-}} \ e^{-i q^+ w^{-}} \ e^{-iw^- p^+} \ e^{i \x\cdot \p} \ e^{iy^-k^+} \ e^{-i\y\cdot \k} \ \theta(p^+) \ \theta(k^+) \ \big[ ig t^a \ A^{a}_{j}(x)\big] \nn \times \ \ \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(p^+ - k^+)} \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \Big[ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \Big( \frac{\gamma^-}{2} - \frac{\gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} + \frac{m}{2k^+}\Big)\Big] U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z,z^-)\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-376:
In that case, the integrations over $z^{-}$, $w^{-}$ can be performed trivially, one obtains  egin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \!  rac{dq^+}{2 \pi}  rac{i}{2q^+} \int \!  rac{dp^+}{2\pi} \int \!  rac{dk^+}{2\pi} \int \!  rac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \!  rac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \ e^{-iq^{+}x^{-}} e^{i \x\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^-k^+} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k} 
n 	imes 2 \pi \delta(p^+ - k^+) 	heta(p^+) \ 	heta(k^+) \  ig[ig t^a \ A^{a}_{j}(x) ig] \ 2\pi \delta(q^+ - p^+) 
n 	imes \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \Big[ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \Big(  rac{\gamma^-}{2} -  rac{\gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} +  rac{m}{2k^+}\Big)\Big] \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation} It is now also straight forward to integrate over $q^+$, $p^+$ and $\p$, which yields to  egin{equation}S_{F}(x,y)  ig|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \!  rac{dk^+}{2\pi} \  rac{	heta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-i(x^- -y^-)k^+} \int \!  rac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k}\ e^{i\z\cdot\k} \ \delta^2(\x - \z)  ig[ig t^a \ \A^{a}_{j}(x) ig] 
n 	imes \Big[  rac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^-}{2} -  rac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} +  rac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ m}{2k^+} \Big] \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation} Finally, rewriting $\k^l$ as a derivative acting on the phase and then integrating over it, we get the final expression for the first contribution as  egin{equation}\label{transverse x} S_{F}(x,y) ig|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \!  rac{dk^+}{2\pi} \  rac{	heta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-i(x^- -y^-)k^+} \int \! d^{2}\z \ \delta^2(\x - \z) \ \delta^2(\z- \y)  ig[ ig t^a \ \A^{a}_{j}(x) ig] 
n 	imes U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-}) \Big[  rac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^-}{2} +  rac{ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^l}{2k^+} \overleftarrow{ rac{d}{d\y^j}} +  rac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ m}{2k^+} \Big]\end{equation} The next contribution comes from inserting the transverse component of the background field on the $y^+$ analogue to the first contribution.
elements.formula-2411-158: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) |_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dq^+}{2 \pi} \frac{i}{2q^+} \int \! \frac{dp^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \int \! \frac{d^{2}\p}{(2 \pi)^2} \ \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \ e^{-iq^{+}x^{-}} e^{i \x\cdot\p} \ e^{iy^-k^+} \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k} \nn \times 2 \pi \delta(p^+ - k^+) \theta(p^+) \ \theta(k^+) \ \big[ig t^a \ A^{a}_{j}(x)\big] \ 2\pi \delta(q^+ - p^+) \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\p - \k)} \ \Big[ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \Big( \frac{\gamma^-}{2} - \frac{\gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} + \frac{m}{2k^+}\Big)\Big] \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-159: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \ \frac{\theta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-i(x^- -y^-)k^+} \int \! \frac{d^{2}\k}{(2 \pi)^2} \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\y\cdot\k}\ e^{i\z\cdot\k} \ \delta^2(\x - \z) \big[ig t^a \ \A^{a}_{j}(x)\big] \nn \times \Big[ \frac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^-}{2} - \frac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^l \k^l}{2k^+} + \frac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ m}{2k^+} \Big] \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-})\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-160: \begin{equation}\label{transverse x} S_{F}(x,y)\big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2\pi} \ \frac{\theta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-i(x^- -y^-)k^+} \int \! d^{2}\z \ \delta^2(\x - \z) \ \delta^2(\z- \y) \big[ ig t^a \ \A^{a}_{j}(x)\big] \nn \times U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-}) \Big[ \frac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^-}{2} + \frac{ \gamma^j \gamma^+ \gamma^l}{2k^+} \overleftarrow{\frac{d}{d\y^j}} + \frac{i \gamma^j \gamma^+ m}{2k^+} \Big]\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-377: In this case the propagator can be written as \begin{equation}\label{eq:contr_y+_side_1} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ S_{F}(x,w)\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q} \ \big(-ig \gamma^j t^a\big) \A^{a}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) |_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{Inst,q}\end{equation} where we have an instantaneous quark propagator from $y$ to $w$.
elements.formula-2411-161: \begin{equation}\label{eq:contr_y+_side_1} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ S_{F}(x,w)\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q} \ \big(-ig \gamma^j t^a\big) \A^{a}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) |_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{Inst,q}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-378: At point $w$ the transverse component of the background field is inserted and then we have a quark propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background from $w$ to $x$.
elements.formula-2411-162: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ \int \! \frac{d^{3}\up}{(2 \pi)^3} \ \int \! \frac{d^{3}\uq}{(2 \pi)^3} \ e^{-ix \cdot \check{q} + iw \cdot \check{p}} \int \! dz^- \ e^{iz^-(q^+ - p^+)} \ \int \! d^{2}\z \ e^{-i\z\cdot(\q - \p)} \nn \times \frac{(\check{\slashed{q} }+ m)}{2q^+} \gamma^{+} \ \theta(q^+) \ \theta(p^+) \ U_{F}(x^+,w^+,\z,z^-) \frac{(\check{\slashed{p}} + m)}{2p^+} \big[ (-ig \gamma^j t^a) A^{a}_{j}(w)\big] \nn \times \delta^{3}(w - y) \gamma^+ \ \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2 \pi} \frac{i}{2k^+} \ e^{-ik^{+}(w^{-} - y^{-})}\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-163: \begin{equation}\label{transverse y} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2 \pi} \frac{\theta(k^+)}{2k^+} \ e^{-i(x^-y^-) k^+} \ \Big[ \frac{-i\gamma^- \gamma^+ \gamma^j}{2} + \frac{\gamma^i \gamma^+ \gamma^j }{2k^+}\overrightarrow{\frac{d}{d\x^i}} - \frac{i\gamma^+ \gamma^j m}{2k^+}) \Big] \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ \delta^2(\x-\z)\ \delta^2(\z -\y) \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z, x^{-}) \ \big[-ig t^a \A^{a}_{j}(y) \big]\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-380: In that case, the propagator reads To compute this contribution, we will use expression, \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y)\big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ \int\! d^{4}w' S_{F}(x,w')\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-} }^{Inst,q} \ (-ig \gamma^{j'} t^a) \A^{a}_{j'}(w') \ S_{F}(w',w) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q} \nn \times (-ig \gamma^j t^b) A^{b}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) \big|_{Eik\, \A^{-}}^{Inst,q}\end{equation} where we have an instantaneous quark propagator from point $y$ to $w$.
elements.formula-2411-164: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y)\big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q} = \int \! d^{4}w \ \int\! d^{4}w' S_{F}(x,w')\big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-} }^{Inst,q} \ (-ig \gamma^{j'} t^a) \A^{a}_{j'}(w') \ S_{F}(w',w) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{q} \nn \times (-ig \gamma^j t^b) A^{b}_{j}(w) \ S_{F}(w,y) \big|_{Eik\, \A^{-}}^{Inst,q}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-381: At point $w$ transverse background field is inserted.
elements.sent-2411-382: Then, we have a quark propagator in a pure $\A^-$ background from point $w$ to $w'$ where another transverse background field is inserted.
elements.sent-2411-383: Finally, we have another instantaneous quark propagator from point $w'$ to $x$.
elements.sent-2411-384: This contribution can be computed by following similar steps and the final result can be written as \begin{equation}\label{transverse xy} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2 \pi} \frac{\theta(k^+)}{2k^{+}} \ e^{-i(x^{-}- y^-)k^+} \big[ig t^a A^{a}_{i}(x)\big] \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ \delta^2(\x - \z) \ \delta^2(\z - \y) \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z,x^{-}) \big[-ig t^b \A^{b}_{j}(y)\Big] \ \Big[\frac{\gamma^{i} \gamma^+ \gamma^j}{2k^+}\Big]\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-165: \begin{equation}\label{transverse xy} S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q} = \int \! \frac{dk^+}{2 \pi} \frac{\theta(k^+)}{2k^{+}} \ e^{-i(x^{-}- y^-)k^+} \big[ig t^a A^{a}_{i}(x)\big] \nn \times \int \! d^{2}\z \ \delta^2(\x - \z) \ \delta^2(\z - \y) \ U_{F}(x^+,y^+,\z,x^{-}) \big[-ig t^b \A^{b}_{j}(y)\Big] \ \Big[\frac{\gamma^{i} \gamma^+ \gamma^j}{2k^+}\Big]\end{equation}
elements.subsection-2411-018: Total inside-to-inside quark propagator in a gluon background field at eikonal order
elements.sent-2411-385: The total inside-to-inside quark propagator at eikonal order for $x^+ > y^+$ can be written as sum of the contributions computed above.
elements.sent-2411-386: Schematically, it reads \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik}^{II,q} = S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q}\end{equation} where each contribution on the right hand side are given in Eqs.
elements.formula-2411-166: \begin{equation}S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik}^{II,q} = S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A^{-}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,\, \A_{\perp x}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,A_{\perp y}}^{II,q} + S_{F}(x,y) \big|_{Eik,A_{\perp xy}}^{II,q}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-387: Using these explicit expressions for each contribution and also using the following relation \begin{equation}\overrightarrow{D}_{\x^i} \ f(x,y) \ \overleftarrow{D}_{\y^j} = \Big(\overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} + ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\Big) \ f(x,y) \ \Big(\overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} - ig t^b A_j^b(y)\Big) \nn = \overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} f(x,y) \overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} + \overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} f(x,y) \big[ - ig t^b \A_j^b(y)\big] + \big[ ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\big] f(x,y) \overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} \nn + \big[ ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\big] f(x,y) \big[ - ig t^b \A_j^b(y)\big]\end{equation} one obtains Eq. \eqref{eq:inside-inside_quark for the inside-to-inside quark propagator at eikonal order.}
elements.formula-2411-167: \begin{equation}\overrightarrow{D}_{\x^i} \ f(x,y) \ \overleftarrow{D}_{\y^j} = \Big(\overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} + ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\Big) \ f(x,y) \ \Big(\overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} - ig t^b A_j^b(y)\Big) \nn = \overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} f(x,y) \overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} + \overrightarrow{\partial}_{\x^i} f(x,y) \big[ - ig t^b \A_j^b(y)\big] + \big[ ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\big] f(x,y) \overleftarrow{\partial}_{\y^j} \nn + \big[ ig t^a \A_i^a(x)\big] f(x,y) \big[ - ig t^b \A_j^b(y)\big]\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-388: Moreover, one can study the case $y^+>x^+$ using the same method, and find the result given in Eq. \eqref{eq:inside-inside-antiquark for the inside-to-inside antiquark propagator at eikonal order.}
elements.section-2411-011: Derivation of the inside-to-after gluon propagator
elements.sent-2411-389: In this Appendix, we present a detailed derivation of the inside-to-after gluon propagator at Eikonal order, i.e. we will consider the gluon propagator for $x^+>L^+/2$ and $-L^+/2<y^+<L^+/2$.
elements.sent-2411-390: For this purpose, we start with the gluon propagator in pure $\A^-$ background at Eikonal order with $z^-$ dependence given in Eq. \eqref{GEikF and consider the case with $-L^+/2<y^+<L^+/2$.}
elements.sent-2411-391: Since the point $y^+$ is inside the medium, we can approximate the phase factor $e^{iy^+\check{k ^-}$ by $1$, as all other contributions coming from the phase brings an extra suppression factor, thus making the expression NEik or beyond.}
elements.sent-2411-392: Moreover, since $x^+>L^+/2$ and $-L^+/2<y^+<L^+/2$, we have necessarily $x^+>y^+$, so that we can drop the instantaneous contribution and the $y^+>x^+$ contribution in Eq. \eqref{GEikF .}
elements.sent-2411-393: Finally, as in the derivation performed inside-to-inside quark propagator, we would like to integrate our expression over $\k$.
elements.sent-2411-394: Thus, we rewrite the factors of $\k$ in the expression as derivates acting on the phase.
elements.sent-2411-396: If one expands the Wilson line around a position $z^-=y^-$, the zeroth term gives contribution at Eikonal order while the first term contributes at NEik order.
elements.sent-2411-397: Since we are interested in deriving the medium contribution to inside-to-after gluon propagator at Eikonal order, one should only keep the zeroth order term in the expansion.
elements.sent-2411-398: In that case, the only $z^-$ dependence appears at the phase which can be integrated trivially and it gives a delta function in $k^+-q^+$.
elements.sent-2411-401: Then from point $w$ to $x$ we have the pure $\A^-$ contribution to the inside-to-after gluon propagator at eikonal order which is given in Eq. \eqref{eq:IA_pure_A_minus_gluon .}
elements.sent-2411-403: The final result is given in \eqref{eq:inside-after_gluon which in the static target field approximation reads Eq. \eqref{IA G_Eik .}}
